back_translation: God blessed Noah and his sons and said to them, "Be fruitful and multiply and fill the earth.

Fear and dread of you will fall on every wild animal of the earth, every bird of the sky, every kind of crawling creature on the ground, and all the fish of the sea. They are delivered into your hand.
Every living and moving thing will be food for you. Just as I gave you the green plants, I now give you all things.
But you must not eat meat that still has its blood—the life of the animal—in it.

And as for your blood, the life of yourselves, I will surely demand an accounting. I will demand it from the hand of every animal, and from the hand of any man, from the hand of his fellow man, I will demand the life of mankind.
Whoever sheds the blood of mankind, by mankind his blood shall be shed, because God made mankind in His image.
But as for you, be fruitful and multiply; spread out across the earth and multiply upon it."
God said to Noah and his sons with him,
"Behold, I am establishing My covenant with you and your descendants who come after you,
and with every living creature that is with you—the birds, the livestock, and every wild animal on the earth—every creature that came out of the ark.
I will establish My covenant with you. Every kind of living thing will not be destroyed by floodwaters again, and there will not be a flood to destroy the earth again."
God said, "This is the sign of the covenant I am setting between Me and you and every living creature with you, for all generations forever:
I will set My bow in the clouds, and it shall be the sign of the covenant between Me and the earth.
Whenever I bring clouds over the earth, and the bow appears in the clouds,
I will remember My covenant between Me and you and every living creature of every kind. The waters shall not become a flood to destroy all flesh again.
When the bow appears in the clouds, I will look upon it and remember the eternal covenant between God and every living creature of every kind on the earth."
God said to Noah, "This is the sign of the covenant I have established between Me and every kind of living creature on the earth."
The sons of Noah who came out of the ark were Shem, Ham, and Japheth. Ham was the father of Canaan.
These three were the sons of Noah, and from them the entire earth was populated.
Noah, a farmer, began to plant a vineyard.
He drank the wine, became drunk, and lay uncovered inside his tent.
Ham, the father of Canaan, saw the nakedness of his father, and he went out and told his two brothers who were outside.

Shem and Japheth took a garment and placed it on both their shoulders, and walking backward, they covered their father's nakedness. Their faces were turned the other way so they would not see their father's nakedness.
When Noah awoke from his drunkenness, he learned what his youngest son had done to him.
He said, "Cursed be Canaan! He shall be the lowliest slave to his brothers."
He also said, "Blessed be the LORD, the God of Shem; may Canaan be a slave to Shem.
May God enlarge the territory of Japheth; may he dwell in the tents of Shem; and may Canaan be his slave."
After the flood, Noah lived 350 more years.
Noah lived a total of 950 years, and then he died.
